chapter_title.en: Chapter 92 – The perfume for the one in Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/312)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/312)

parts.text: مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ هِلَالٍ

text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يَمَسُّ الطِّيبَ وَ هُوَ نَائِمٌ لَا يَعْلَمُ قَالَ يَغْسِلُهُ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ وَ عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يَدْهُنُهُ الْحَلَالُ بِالدُّهْنِ الطَّيِّبِ وَ الْمُحْرِمُ لَا يَعْلَمُ مَا عَلَيْهِ قَالَ يَغْسِلُهُ أَيْضاً وَ لْيَحْذَرْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Abdullah Bin Hilal, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the one in Ihraam touching the perfume and he is asleep. Heasws said: ‘He should wash it, and there is nothing upon him (to expiate)’. And about the one in Ihraam being massaged by the one not in Ihraam, with the aromatic oil, and the one in Ihraam does not know what is upon him. Heasws said: ‘He should wash it as well, and let him be cautious’.
en.sarwar: 15. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Abbas ibn Ma‘ruf from Ali ibn Mahziyar who has said the following: “I once asked ibn abu ‘Umayr about apple, citron and lotus and things with sweet fragrance. He said, ‘Hold back from smelling while you eat them.’” (It seems like it is ibn abu ‘Umayr’s Fatwa)
